codeReview.summary: The dropdown UI and keyboard mechanics are largely implemented, but there are two blocking correctness issues: policy writes target `request` cells instead of the feature’s specified `respond` cells, and the internal ownership tracking can overwrite later user manual overrides when switching defaults.

issues.description: Default-policy application dispatches `direction: "request"` for both "Deny by default" and "Allow known peers" (also keyed as `:request:` in tracking), but the feature description specifies `Deny by default = respond.*=deny` for inbound-request handling. This wiring cannot enforce the described reactive-denial semantics.

The component unsets every previously dropdown-applied key on each selection change (`priorKeys`), while `hasUserOverride` treats keys in `defaultAppliedKeys` as non-user-owned. If a user manually changes a cell after a default was applied, that cell can still be in `priorKeys` and gets unset on the next default switch, clobbering manual overrides and violating the 'peers with overrides are unaffected' invariant.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission artifacts disagree on policy direction semantics for this dropdown, which likely contributed to incorrect direction wiring and tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json:2120 defines "Deny by default" as `respond.*=deny`; validation-contract.md:590-592 asserts `effective_policy.request.*`; architecture.md:76 states inbound denial checks `respond.sign`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The ui-screen-worker skill requirement around `ui-builder` appears too rigid/unclear for component-level UI changes, and workers can mark followedProcedure=true without invoking it.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/ui-screen-worker/SKILL.md:22 requires invoking `ui-builder` for Paper-derived UI work; transcript skeleton for worker session c0b287e1-4acc-4139-a744-d81ae6b4d7b1 shows skills `mission-worker-base` and `ui-screen-worker` only; handoff JSON lines 124-126 records `followedProcedure: true` with no deviations.

summary: FAIL: Reviewed the feature handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit 7d5e7cb. The implementation does not fully satisfy the feature contract due to direction-mapping and override-clobbering bugs in DefaultPolicyDropdown.
